title: pouteria robusta var. typica
intro: Pouteria robusta var. typica, also known as the Kamani or Ceylon Ironwood, is a tropical tree species native to the Pacific Islands and Southeast Asia, valued for its medicinal and timber properties. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, and its bark contains various compounds with antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which have been found to possess wound-healing and anti-cancer activities. The health benefits of Pouteria robusta var. typica include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and potentially inhibit the growth of cancer cells. In traditional medicine, the bark is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, while modern herbal preparations may involve the use of extracts or essential oils derived from the plant's bark.
